Transcrição Completa: How To Win The Sales Game

 

[00:00] Sales is a game. It's a game of both strategy and reward. Also, failure points that can be booby traps that can really fuck you.

 

[00:08] The game itself is fun and it's meant to be played with ambition, drive, and a willingness to have those rewards be reaped from the game board itself.

 

[00:17] You are now participating as an agency owner in one of the greatest games you could play. It's very high ticket. We get an extremely high profit margin per deal.

 

[00:27] It's extremely fun. It's not that time intensive if you're selling results instead of selling time and effort.

 

[00:34] And of course, there's going to be a lot of people willing to jump on the ship that you're on in the game itself. When I say that, you'll be able to get salespeople quite easily because the margins will be phenomenal for them to get paid on, especially if you're paying on a recurring basis and having the sales people help you manage the account.

 

[00:53] Let's say it's just you to start off. You have no clients and you're wondering what the fuck I'm talking about when I say that it's all a game.

 

[01:00] It's quite simple. Let's say you want to close 10 clients at $5,000 a pop or $10,000 a pop or $1,000 a pop to start whatever it is that fits you and your current confidence and certainty levels.

 

[01:14] You now have an action that you need to take in order to get a result on the game map. You have to have the right actions taken. You have to spend the right amount of time in the right areas.

 

[01:25] And you're going to derive the right results from increasing your odds by pitching the right amount of people. If you need 10 clients, you're likely going to have to pitch 100, maybe 200, 500 people if your closing rate's pretty low.

 

[01:38] When you do so, you're going to find that there's predictability. When you continue outputting enough emails, videos, social media messages, everything to follow up with people as well.

 

[01:49] In that process of you getting your first clients, you're going to find that you make however much money you chose to make.

 

[01:56] You are going to need to reward yourself. Mr. Jordan Belfort used to do this in a very unhealthy way. He's the Wolf of Wall Street, if you're not familiar with him.

 

[02:05] He used to talk about this whenever he would do an enormous deal and as soon as it would close, he would ensure that his brain would be wired to understand that that was an incredible time and that it needed to get that moment again.

 

[02:18] So, he would blow down a line of coke, which I don't encourage you to do. Do not do drugs.

 

[02:23] When you get a deal, it is important that you celebrate. It's important that you walk around and pound your chest, get excited, you know, let the dopamine and the serotonin fire off in your brain.

 

[02:34] Do some push-ups. Get exhilarated. Okay? Jump around, chest bump each other. Do anything to get excited every time that you close a deal.

 

[02:43] But also reward yourself. When I was first getting started, found a nice designer brand that I liked. It's called Y3, a Japanese brand.

 

[02:51] I figured, hey, you know, tall white guy, I'd probably look great in that. Let's let's try it out.

 

[02:56] So, I go to this Japanese shop and it's it's right here in Miami on Lincoln Road and I pick up this sweater and I remember I was just intimidated by this sucker. It was $300 and I had $400 in my pocket.

 

[03:09] Not that that was the only money that I had. I just had four Benjamins on me, right?

 

[03:14] So, I go into the store. I have this sweater in my hand. I asked the guy how much it was. He said it was 300 bucks. And I was like, man, let's do it. I want to level up.

 

[03:24] So, I give the guy 300 bucks. I get this sweater and I intentionally just as I heard Jordan Belfort had done, you know, made myself feel great and just got excited, you know, let the chills run up and down me and really I used it as a wealth trigger to continue reminding myself that that was the level that I wanted to aspire to at the time.

 

[03:46] And eventually, depending on the season, of course, I would go in and buy almost all of the season or just a bunch of different clothes from there.

 

[03:53] Every time I would go and close a deal, I used it as my reward circuitry inside of my head to let myself know, hey, if I close a deal, I'm going to feel great and I'm going to look great, too.

 

[04:04] So, that was a great cycle for me that continued to inspire me rather than be bogged down while you're getting all the rejection from participating in sales.

 

[04:13] Long story short, you got to create a reward for yourself, even if it's just you when you're first getting started.

 

[04:19] The best thing to do, and it's much easier to do this and affordable, I found I have higher standards than what most of my sales guys will.

 

[04:28] So, when I say that, you know, Y3 is a little more expensive than what I can reward my sales team with.

 

[04:34] I've had all kinds of different sales people that I can do things like $50 gift cards for a specific restaurant that I know they like.

 

[04:42] Maybe I take them out to lunch with me and let them ask me a bunch of questions because I'm a mentor to everybody that works for me.

 

[04:49] In addition to that, maybe I can spend a little bit of money for an extra bonus on the person's birthday or every time they close a deal.

 

[04:56] Not every time of course, but maybe after they close a certain amount of deals, like let's say the person closes three deals in a week. What kind of reward are they going to get?

 

[05:05] Let's say they close five deals in a month and you're closing high ticket. You know, how much more are you going to pay them?

 

[05:11] If you pay them 15% at bringing in whatever, five deals a month, pay them 20% if they bring in eight. You see what I'm saying?

 

[05:19] Pay out more money. Buy cool things. Here in Miami is a great example. We got the port of Miami over here and there's a big cruise ship area where I think for 50 bucks or 100 bucks maybe you can go to the Bahamas for the day.

 

[05:32] So, anybody in my staff here locally or just other people that work with me, sometimes as a reward, I'll make sure I incentivize them to know that there's a bunch of cool stuff they can do and I'll send them on a helicopter ride or send them on a cruise ship to the Bahamas for the day.

 

[05:46] I've only done that twice. So many cost effective ways to give people cool stuff.

 

[05:51] There's also things you can do with clothing. People, especially the younger people, love clothes. I know I do, right? You want to look great, so does your team.

 

[06:00] Inspire people by giving them nice shit. You can even go as far as rewarding their family members if they have extreme family values while they're working with you.

 

[06:09] So, you can not just reward them, but you can help them reward their family, which might be why they're working with you in the first place.

 

[06:16] Find out what your people's values are and create a reward-based incentive. It's all game.

 

[06:22] You create the game, though, once you duplicate yourself into salespeople. You have to manage the game for them because they're not going to self-manage a game.

 

[06:31] You can self-manage a game for yourself when you're first getting started. This will help you reward yourself. It'll help motivate you. It'll help create a positive cycle of action.

 

[06:41] And it'll reinforce a bunch of great feelings rather than every time you close a deal. Maybe you close it because you were stressed out, needed to pay some bills, so you get relief like you know, you don't want that rather than having an extremely positive I want that again kind of attitude.

 

[06:58] So, make sure you handle this wisely as this is some neuroscience logic. See you in the next video.

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

10 clientes como meta inicial

$5,000, $10,000 ou $1,000 por cliente (dependendo do nível de confiança)

100-500 pessoas para pitch (para fechar 10 clientes)

$300 (sweater Y3 como recompensa pessoal)

$400 em dinheiro no bolso

$50 gift cards para equipe

$50-$100 para viagem às Bahamas

15% de comissão base

20% de comissão para 8+ deals/mês

3 deals/semana como marco de recompensa

5 deals/mês como benchmark

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Sistema de recompensas neurológicas (dopamina/serotonina)

Celebração física imediata após cada venda

Wealth triggers (gatilhos de riqueza) através de compras simbólicas

Escala de comissionamento progressivo

Recompensas personalizadas baseadas em valores individuais

Mentoria como forma de recompensa

Incentivos para família dos vendedores

Follow-up consistente via emails, vídeos e mensagens

 

ESTRUTURA:

Modelo high ticket com alta margem de lucro

Venda de resultados (não tempo/esforço)

Pagamento recorrente para vendedores

Vendedores ajudam na gestão de contas

Sistema de duplicação do fundador em vendedores

Gestão ativa do "jogo" de vendas pela liderança

Recompensas escalonadas por performance

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Margens extremamente altas por deal

Modelo recorrente de pagamento

ROI positivo em recompensas ($50-$300)

Comissionamento entre 15-20% dependendo de volume

Baixa intensidade de tempo quando estruturado corretamente

 

DURAÇÃO: ~7 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional/motivacional direto para câmera
